sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_inet (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip_address INET NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE test_strings (str_id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings (value) VALUES ('example string'), ('another example'), ('test string with trailing aaaa');
SELECT rtrim(value, 'a') AS trimmed_value FROM test_strings WHERE rtrim(value, 'a') << (SELECT ip_address FROM test_inet WHERE id = 1);
SELECT rtrim(value, 'e') AS trimmed_value, COUNT(*) FROM test_strings GROUP BY rtrim(value, 'e') HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT rt.trimmed_value FROM (SELECT rtrim(value, 'example') AS trimmed_value FROM test_strings) rt WHERE rt.trimmed_value << inet '192.168.1.0/24';
SELECT DISTINCT (rtrim(value, '') << (SELECT inet '192.168.1.1' UNION ALL SELECT inet '192.168.1.2')) AS comparison FROM test_strings;
SELECT rt1.value FROM test_strings rt1 JOIN (SELECT rtrim(value, 'g') AS trimmed_value FROM test_strings) rt2 ON rt1.value << rt2.trimmed_value WHERE rt1.value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TEMPORARY TABLE temp_ips AS SELECT ip_address FROM test_inet WHERE ip_address << inet '192.168.1.0/24';

SELECT rt.value, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_ips WHERE temp_ips.ip_address << inet '192.168.1.1') AS contained_count FROM test_strings rt WHERE rt.value IS NOT NULL AND rtrim(rt.value, ' ') IS NOT NULL;
